author: Devon Carter, RN / Telemetry tech S. Ahmed; reviewed by Dr. Priya Anand (Medicine)
content:
TELEMETRY EVENT DOCUMENTATION — POD#1

Reason for note: Document atrial fibrillation runs captured on continuous telemetry over the preceding 24 hours and reviewed in detail with the monitoring technician.

DETAILED REVIEW OF TELEMETRY (last 24 h):
- THREE discrete episodes of ATRIAL FIBRILLATION were captured.
- Each episode lasted approximately 15-20 SECONDS and terminated spontaneously (self-limited), with return to normal sinus rhythm between episodes.
- During the runs, the ventricular response was irregular and briefly increased to ~115-120 bpm; baseline sinus rate between runs ~70-80.
- The patient was ASYMPTOMATIC during all three episodes (no palpitations, chest pain, lightheadedness, or dyspnea reported; sleeping during two of the three).
- No associated hypotension; blood pressure remained at her baseline during and after the runs.
- No ventricular ectopy of concern; no pauses; no high-grade AV block.

Nursing actions: Episodes reviewed with telemetry tech and flagged to the Medicine team (Dr. Anand) for co-management. 12-lead ECG obtained this morning (IMG003) showed normal sinus rhythm (captured between runs). Patient reassured; continues to feel well.

This note exists to consolidate the objective rhythm data, which is otherwise summarized only briefly in the daily progress notes.
attestation: Electronically documented by Devon Carter, RN; telemetry data reviewed with S. Ahmed (monitor tech) and Dr. Priya Anand (Medicine). 6/11/2027.
